mnemonics: ACID Rain: To ensure data integrity, a transaction must be ACID (Atomic, Consistent, Isolated, Durable).
keyPoints: A transaction is a single logical unit of work that accesses and possibly modifies the contents of a database.
The ACID properties (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) are the key properties of a transaction.
The system keeps track of transactions by writing log records to stable storage.
commonMistakes: Confusing system crash with transaction failure. A system crash requires recovery of the entire system state, while a transaction failure only requires rolling back that single transaction.
Assuming a committed transaction's changes are immediately written to disk. The changes might still be in a buffer and not yet durable until the log is forced.
